title: The Happiness Trap: How to Stop Struggling and Start Living
subtitle: The definitive popular introduction to Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) — the counterintuitive path to well-being through accepting, not avoiding, difficult emotions.
author: Russ Harris
themes: The happiness myth — chasing happiness makes you unhappy
The control agenda — trying to control emotions backfires
Acceptance as the alternative to avoidance
Values-based action as the path to meaning and fulfillment
excerpt: The Happiness Trap challenges the fundamental assumption of modern culture: that happiness is the natural state of being. Russ Harris, a medical doctor turned ACT therapist, argues that the relentless pursuit of happiness actually makes us unhappy. Instead, ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy) offers a counterintuitive path: accept difficult emotions, let go of the struggle, and take action aligned with your values. The result is not constant happiness, but a rich, full, and meaningful life.
